solution_uniqueness_guard.reason: Tests assert on gold-specific implementation details: exact variable names like 'p' in 'if p.exists() {' and 'std::fs::remove_dir_all(&p).unwrap();', 'api_file' in 'if api_file.exists() {', the exact library 'fs_extra::dir::copy', and specific patterns like 'CopyOptions'. tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: Tests test_buggy_pattern_removed, test_fixed_deletes_after_build, test_update_builds_to_tempdir_first, test_safe_deletion_checks_existence, and test_copy_from_tempdir_after_build are purely text-matching assertions (assert pattern in content / assert pattern not in content). While test_compilation_passes and test_syntax_valid do run cargo check, the core fail-to-pass tests are all string-in-file checks with no behavioral execution.

A stub or no-op wouldn't contain these patterns, so they wouldn't pass.

The agent could read the gold patch from the solution directory or from test_outputs.py (which contains the exact string patterns needed) to reverse-engineer the expected code. behavior_in_tests.reason: The instruction requires: (1) cargo check passes - tested by test_compilation_passes, (2) build to temp dir first then safe update - tested by multiple pattern tests. The core behavioral requirements from instruction.md are covered.

informative_test_structure.reason: Tests are well-named (test_compilation_passes, test_buggy_pattern_removed, test_fixed_deletes_after_build, etc.), have docstrings explaining their purpose, and are logically grouped by concern.

structured_data_schema.reason: This task modifies Rust source code; it does not produce structured data like JSON/CSV/API/DB.
